text.title: Model Scopes, Callbacks, and Eager Loading
text.summary: Named scopes for simple primitives, callback rules, and the eager-load-by-default principle.

rule.statement: Eager-load at the controller boundary, never in views. A view triggering a query is invisible in code review.
rule.rationale: View-triggered queries are rendered as template output, not as SQL log entries in the request cycle that code reviewers check. Eager loading in controllers makes query cost reviewable.

rule.statement: Avoid default_scope; use named scopes (scope :active) and force callers to opt in.
rule.rationale: A default_scope { where(deleted_at: nil) } is invisible at every call site. Agents debugging "missing data" rarely think to call withoutGlobalScopes — it costs an hour per incident.

rule.statement: Use find_each / in_batches for any loop over 1k+ records; never User.all.each.
rule.rationale: User.all.each loads the entire result set into memory. find_each streams in batches of 1000, keeping memory constant.

example.code:
class User < ApplicationRecord has_one :profile, dependent: :destroy has_many :orders, dependent: :nullify has_many :roles, through: :user_roles
validates :email, presence: true, uniqueness: { case_sensitive: false } validates :name, presence: true, length: { minimum: 2, maximum: 100 }
# Named scopes — callers opt in scope :active, -> { where(active: true) } scope :admins, -> { joins(:roles).where(roles: { name: 'admin' }) } scope :recent, -> { where('created_at > ?', 30.days.ago) }
before_validation :normalize_email after_create_commit :send_welcome_email
private
def normalize_email self.email = email&.downcase&.strip end
def send_welcome_email UserMailer.welcome(self).deliver_later end end

antipattern.description: update_all used on audited or paper-trailed models.
antipattern.reason: update_all bypasses callbacks silently — counter_cache, paper_trail, and audit logs break without any error. Use update! per record or explicitly disable callbacks with a comment explaining why.
